title: Creative Europe at the 2026 Brussels book fair
content:
Creative Europe at the 2026 Brussels book fair

csudaga Fri, 03/20/2026 - 14:25

Highlights of the fair

Inaugural ceremony

European Commissioner Glenn Micallef will speak at the opening of the event on 25 March, showcasing the EU's commitment to supporting the book industry and promoting reading.

Support for Ukraine

Commissioner Micallef will also participate in the distribution of Creative Europe-funded books to Ukrainian children.

Engaging discussions

Meet authors, translators, publishers, booksellers, and librarians to explore topics like literary translation
creator: csudaga

title: 46 projects selected for funding under the 2025 Circulation of European Literary Works call
content:
46 projects selected for funding under the 2025 Circulation of European Literary Works call

csudaga Tue, 03/17/2026 - 11:08

46 projects, comprising a total of 499 works of fiction, have been selected for translation from 36 into 24 languages, with a prevalence of youth literature and novels. In the next three years, these works will be published, promoted, and made available to readers across Europe and beyond, showcasing the rich linguistic and cultural diversity of European literature.

Creative Europe: promoting collaboration and exchange

Out of 189 submissions, 46 proposals were select

title: 4 cities shortlisted for the European Capital of Culture 2031 in Spain
content:
4 cities shortlisted for the European Capital of Culture 2031 in Spain

csudaga Fri, 03/13/2026 - 11:04

The panel of independent experts recommended the shortlist following a meeting held in Madrid from 9 to 13 March 2026.

Spain will host a European Capital of Culture for the fourth time, after Madrid in 1992, Santiago de Compostela in 2000, Salamanca in 2002 and San Sebastián in 2016.

Shortlisted cities

Cáceres

Cáceres chose to focus on the concept of “Transcultura” and celebrate diversity, unity and coexistence. While showcasing the cultural richness and herita

title: EU boosts preparedness of cultural heritage sector for crisis and conflict
content:
EU boosts preparedness of cultural heritage sector for crisis and conflict

csudaga Wed, 02/18/2026 - 15:02

Enhancing preparedness across the EU

Europe's cultural heritage is increasingly threatened by natural and human-induced disasters, exacerbated by climate change. In recent years, crisis and conflict have also increasingly impacted cultural heritage in Europe and beyond.

In response to this, the European Commission’s Directorate-General for Education, Youth, Sport, and Culture launched the READY project
creator: csudaga

title: Europe’s 7 most endangered sites of 2026 revealed
content:
Europe’s 7 most endangered sites of 2026 revealed

On 26 February, Europa Nostra announced the list of the 7 most endangered monuments and heritage sites in Europe for 2026:

  • Katapola Village and Ancient City of Minoa, Greece

title: 13 sites to receive the European Heritage Label
content:
13 sites to receive the European Heritage Label

csudaga Tue, 02/17/2026 - 11:21

The European Heritage Label (EHL) is a unique initiative supported by Creative Europe to promote a shared understanding of European history grounded in fundamental rights, democratic values and pluralism.

A panel of independent experts chose the 13 sites from a shortlist of 21 candidates that member states selected. Each site symbolises a unique piece of Europe's diverse history and integration, embodying the core values at the heart of Europe.</
creator: csudaga

title: 2026 Creative Europe call for European cooperation projects open
content:
2026 Creative Europe call for European cooperation projects open

csudaga Tue, 02/17/2026 - 10:08

With a budget of around €60 million, the action will foster cooperation between organisations active in the field of culture, increasing the cross-border dimension of creation and circulation of European artistic content. It will also encourage the development, experimentation, dissemination, and application of new and innovative practices.
